Appearance
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

The 2023 AFC Asian Cup was an international football tournament held in Qatar from 12 January to 10 February 2024. The 24 national teams involved in the tournament were required to register a squad with a minimum of 18 players and a maximum of 26 players, at least three of whom had to be goalkeepers. Only players in these squads were eligible to take part in the tournament.

Before announcing their final squads, teams had to send AFC a provisional squad of 18 to 50 players; each country's final squad had to be submitted at least ten days prior to the first match of the tournament (Regulation 26.7.3).[1] Replacement of players was permitted until six hours before the team's first Asian Cup game (Regulation 26.9).[1] In December 2023, AFC approved the increase of the maximum number of players included on the final list from 23 to 26 (Regulation 26.7).[1] On 4 January 2024, the AFC published the final lists with squad numbers.[2]

The age listed for each player is as of 12 January 2024, the first day of the tournament. The numbers of caps and goals listed for each player do not include any matches played after the start of tournament. Each club listed here are clubs which last represented by players before heading into tournament and nationality for each club reflects the national association (not the league) to which the club is affiliated. A flag is included for coaches that are of a different nationality than their own national team.

Group A

[edit]

Qatar

[edit]

Qatar announced a 27-men preliminary squad on 22 December 2023.[3] On 30 December, Osamah Al-Tairi withdrew injured and was replaced by Ismaeel Mohammad.[4] On 2 January 2024, Mohammed Muntari withdrew injured and was replaced by Khaled Mohammed.[5] The final squad was announced on 3 January.[6]

Coach: Tintín Márquez

China

[edit]

China announced their final squad on 12 December 2023.[7] On 21 December, Nico Yennaris withdrew from the squad for personal reasons and was replaced by Xu Xin.[8]

Tajikistan

[edit]

Tajikistan announced a 27-men preliminary squad on 12 December 2023.[9] The final squad was announced on 4 January 2024.[2] On 12 January, Amirbek Juraboev withdrew injured and was replaced by Komron Tursunov.[10]

Lebanon

[edit]

Lebanon announced a 31-men preliminary squad on 16 December 2023.[11] The final squad was announced on 30 December.[12] On 6 January 2024, George Felix Melki withdrew injured and was replaced by Khalil Khamis.[13]

Uzbekistan

[edit]

Uzbekistan announced a 28-men preliminary squad on 30 December 2023.[15] The final squad was announced on 4 January 2024.[2] On 11 January, Husniddin Aliqulov withdrew injured and was replaced by Shokhboz Umarov.[16]

Syria

[edit]

Syria announced a 33-men preliminary squad on 20 December 2023.[17] The final squad was announced on 31 December.[18] On 2 January 2024, Mardik Mardikian withdrew injured and was replaced by Alaa Al Dali.[19] On 4 January, Mohammed Osman withdrew injured and was replaced by Mouhamad Anez.[20] On 10 January, Mohammad Al Hallak withdrew injured and was replaced by Mohammad Al Marmour.[21]

Coach: Héctor Cúper

[edit]

Iran

[edit]

United Arab Emirates announced a 31-men preliminary squad on 22 December 2023.[26] The final squad was announced on 4 January 2024.[2] On 13 January, Khaled Tawhid withdrew injured and was replaced by Hassan Hamza.[27]

Indonesia

[edit]

Indonesia announced a 29-men preliminary squad on 19 December 2023.[33] The squad was reduced to 28 players on 20 December as Yance Sayuri withdrew injured.[34] The final squad was announced on 4 January 2024.[2] On 11 January, Saddil Ramdani was replaced by Adam Alis.[35] On 14 January, Syahrul Trisna withdrew injured and was replaced by Nadeo Argawinata.[36]

Vietnam

[edit]

Vietnam announced a 34-men preliminary squad on 25 December 2023.[39] Phạm Văn Luân was added to the squad on 28 December.[40] Nguyễn Thành Chung and Hoàng Văn Toản withdrew injured on 29 December and were replaced by Bùi Tiến Dũng and Hồ Tấn Tài. On 31 December, Đặng Văn Lâm, Bùi Tiến Dũng, Nguyễn Đức Chiến and Nguyễn Thanh Nhàn withdrew injured.[41] On 3 January 2024, Quế Ngọc Hải and Nguyễn Tiến Linh withdrew injured. The final squad was announced on 4 January.[2] On the same day, Nguyễn Hoàng Đức withdrew injured and was replaced by Nguyễn Văn Trường. On 12 January, Triệu Việt Hưng was replaced by Lê Ngọc Bảo.[42]

Group F

[edit]

Saudi Arabia

[edit]

Saudi Arabia announced a 30-men preliminary squad on 30 December 2023.[49] The final squad was announced on 2 January 2024.[50] On 13 January, Nawaf Al-Aqidi, Abbas Al-Hassan and Ayman Yahya were removed from the squad for disciplinary reasons and were replaced by Mohammed Al-Rubaie, Mohammed Al-Breik and Talal Haji.[51] On 16 January 2024, Fahad Al-Muwallad withdrew injured and was replaced by Rayane Hamidou.[52]

Thailand

[edit]

Thailand announced their final squad on 3 January 2024.[53] On 7 January, Ekanit Panya declined his call-up for the tournament and was replaced by Picha Autra.[54]

Kyrgyzstan

[edit]

Kyrgyzstan announced a 32-men preliminary squad on 1 December 2023.[55] On 25 December, Azim Azarov was added to the squad. On 30 December, Erbol Atabayev withdrew from the squad with injury. The final squad was announced on 4 January 2024.[2] On 16 January, Kurmanbek Nurlanbekov was replaced by Alimardon Shukurov. Following the replacement, Adil Kadyrzhanov’s number was switched from 22 to 4 and Shukurov was given the number 22 shirt.[56]

Coach: Štefan Tarkovič

Oman

[edit]

Oman announced a 29-men preliminary squad on 10 December 2023.[57] On 19 December, Muhammad Al-Amiri withdrew with an injury and was replaced by Juma Al-Habsi.[58] The final squad was announced on 4 January 2024.[2]

1  Argentina Héctor Cúper (Syria)
 Australia Graham Arnold
 France Philippe Troussier (Vietnam)
 Germany Jürgen Klinsmann (South Korea)
 Iran Amir Ghalenoei
 Italy Roberto Mancini (Saudi Arabia)
 Montenegro Miodrag Radulović (Lebanon)
 Morocco Hussein Ammouta (Jordan)
 Norway Jørn Andersen (Hong Kong)
 Portugal Paulo Bento (United Arab Emirates)
 Serbia Aleksandar Janković (China)
 Slovakia Štefan Tarkovič (Kyrgyzstan)
 Slovenia Srečko Katanec (Uzbekistan)
 Tunisia Makram Daboub (Palestine)

  48. ^ "Pizzi calls up Hussain for Dubai training camp". News of Bahrain. 8 January 2024. Retrieved 14 January 2024.

  51. ^ "— المدير الفني روبرتو مانشيني يضم الثلاثي " محمد البريك ، طلال حاجي ، محمد اليامي " لقائمة الأخضر النهائية لكأس آسيا 2023" [Roberto Mancini includes the trio “Mohamed Al-Breik, Talal Haji, and Mohamed Al-Yami” in the final Green List for the 2023 Asian Cup]. Twitter (in Arabic). Saudi Arabia Football Federation. 13 January 2024. Retrieved 13 January 2024.
  52. ^ "المدير الفني للمنتخب الوطني يضم " ريان حامد " بدلاً من " فهد المولد" [The national team’s technical director includes “Rayane Hamidou” instead of “Fahad Al-Muwallad”]. Twitter (in Arabic). Saudi Arabia Football Federation. 16 January 2024. Retrieved 16 January 2024.
  53. ^ "#OFFICIAL ประกาศรายชื่อ 26 แข้งช้างศึก ชุดลุยศึก เอเชียน คัพ 2023 รอบสุดท้าย ที่ประเทศกาตาร์" [#OFFICIAL Announcing the 26 War Elephants in the squad for the 2023 Asian Cup finals in Qatar]. Facebook (in Thai). Thailand Football Association. 3 January 2024. Retrieved 3 January 2024.
  54. ^ "🚨 #BREAKING : เอกนิษฐ์ ปัญญา ขอถอนตัวจากทีมชาติไทย ชุดลุยเอเชียน คัพ 2023 - อิชิอิ เรียก พิชา เสียบแทน" [🚨 #BREAKING : Ekanit Panya requests to withdraw from the Thai national team's Asian Cup 2023 squad - Ishii calls Picha to replace him]. Facebook (in Thai). Thailand Football Association. 7 January 2024. Retrieved 7 January 2024.
  55. ^ "РАСШИРЕННЫЙ СОСТАВ НАЦИОНАЛЬНОЙ СБОРНОЙ НА УТС В РАМКАХ ПОДГОТОВКИ К КУБКУ АЗИИ 2023" [EXTENDED COMPOSITION OF THE NATIONAL TEAM AT THE TCU AS PART OF PREPARATIONS FOR THE 2023 ASIAN CUP] (in Russian). Kyrgyz Football Union. 1 December 2023. Retrieved 23 December 2023.
  56. ^ "ФИНАЛЬНЫЙ СОСТАВ НАЦИОНАЛЬНОЙ СБОРНОЙ НА КУБОК АЗИИ 2023" [FINAL TEAM FOR THE 2023 ASIAN CUP] (in Russian). Kyrgyz Football Union. 16 January 2024. Retrieved 19 January 2024.
  57. ^ "📋 قائمة منتخبنا الوطني الأول للمعسكرين الداخلي والخارجي في دولة الإمارات العربية المتحدة" [📋 The list of our first national team for internal and external camps in the United Arab Emirates] (in Arabic). Oman Football Association. 10 December 2023. Retrieved 30 December 2023.
  58. ^ "الجهاز الفني للأحمر يقرر استبعاد محمد العامري للإصابة واستدعاء جمعة الحبسي" [The technical body of Al-Hamar decides to exclude injured Mohamed Al-Amari and calls up Juma Al-Habsi] (in Arabic). Oman Football Association. 19 December 2023. Retrieved 30 December 2023.
